name: the primitive type called mime

VINIT.justification: The zero-dimensional point before topological extension.

TANCH.justification: The global boundary that contains the two-cycle system.

AFWD.justification: Directed movement along a single cycle path.

AREV.justification: Reversal of orientation within the cycle.

CLINK.justification: Sequential joining of arc segments into a continuous loop.

IMSCRIB.justification: The central node where the manifold recognizes its own crossing.

FSPLIT.justification: The node where a single path splits into two distinct cycles.

FFUSE.justification: The node where the two cycles return to the common intersection.

EVALT.justification: The first loop of the figure-eight representing the affirmative path.

EVALF.justification: The second loop of the figure-eight representing the alternative path.

ENGAGR.justification: The point belonging to both cycles simultaneously without resolution.

IFIX.justification: The permanent record of the genus or winding number.

steps.domain_action: establish the initial vertex at the origin
identify the vertex as the potential intersection point
initiate the bifurcation into two distinct directional arcs
anchor the primary arc to the positive cycle
extend the primary arc to form a closed loop
anchor the secondary arc to the negative cycle
extend the secondary arc with inverted orientation
hold the state at the intersection where both cycles meet
reconstitute the two cycles into the singular intersection node
compose the resulting figure-eight into a single manifold
record the two-cycle topology as a permanent structural type

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the transformation is a homeostatic topological rearrangement
